label: YouTube Data
description: Search and read videos, channels, playlists, and comments from the YouTube Data API v3.

form.title: Connect YouTube Data
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to search and read YouTube content on your behalf.

methods.label: Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Opens a browser-based Google consent screen so Anton can read YouTube videos, channels, playlists, and comments. Requires a Google Cloud OAuth client you bring yourself — setup takes about 5 minutes.
methods.how_to:
> **Pick the OAuth client type that matches your Anton shell:**
>
> - **Desktop app (Anton.app on macOS / Windows)** → in *Application type*, pick **Desktop app**. Google auto-allows loopback redirects, so no redirect-URI registration is needed.
> - **Web (browser-hosted Anton)** → pick **Web application** instead, then under *Authorized redirect URIs* add `http://127.0.0.1:26866/v1/connectors/oauth/callback` (substitute your server origin if you're behind a reverse proxy or running on a non-default port).
>
> The walkthrough below was written for the desktop path. If you're on web, read every "Desktop app" reference as "Web application" and make sure the redirect URI above is registered before clicking **Submit**.

## Sign in with Google — Bring Your Own OAuth Client

Anton doesn't ship a hosted OAuth client for YouTube yet, so you'll create one in Google Cloud Console (free, ~5 minutes).

### 1. Enable the YouTube Data API

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and pick or create a project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library**, search for **YouTube Data API v3**, and click **Enable**.

### 2. Configure the OAuth consent screen

1. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen**.
2. Choose **External** (or **Internal** for Workspace orgs) and click **Create**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`) and your email, then click **Save and Continue**.
4. On the **Scopes** step, add:
   - `youtube.readonly`
   - `youtube.force-ssl`
   - `userinfo.email`
5. Under **Test users**, add your Google account email, then finish the wizard.

### 3. Create an OAuth client ID

1. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
2. Select **Desktop app** as the application type.
3. Give it a name like `Anton Desktop` and click **Create**.
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown in the dialog.

### 4. Paste into Anton

1. Paste the Client ID and Client secret into the fields below.
2. Click **Submit**. Anton opens your browser to Google's consent screen.
3. Sign in, review the permissions, and click **Continue**.
4. Anton stores the resulting refresh token locally — your credentials never leave your machine.

### Notes

- Refresh tokens are long-lived but will expire if your app stays in **Testing** mode and is unused for 7 days. Publish the consent screen to avoid this.
- To revoke access, visit [Third-party apps with account access](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove the entry.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; copied from the same credentials dialog in Cloud Console.

methods.label: API Key (read-only, public data only)
methods.description: Use a Google Cloud API key to query public YouTube data without signing in. Simpler to set up but restricted to unauthenticated public content — no private videos, subscriptions, or user-specific data.
methods.how_to:
## Create a YouTube Data API Key

API keys work for public, read-only YouTube data (videos, channels, playlists, public comments). They cannot access private content or act on behalf of a user.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and pick or create a project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library**, search for **YouTube Data API v3**, and click **Enable**.
3. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → API key**.
4. Google generates the key immediately — click **Copy**.
5. (Optional but recommended) Click **Restrict key**:
   - Under **API restrictions**, select **Restrict key** and choose **YouTube Data API v3**.
   - Under **Application restrictions**, consider limiting to your IP or referrer.
6. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- API keys have no expiry by default but can be deleted or regenerated in Cloud Console at any time.
- The free quota is 10,000 units per day. Heavy searches or large list operations consume units quickly — check **APIs & Services → Quotas** to monitor usage.
- If you need access to private videos or user account data, use **Sign in with Google** instead.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Restrict it to YouTube Data API v3 for safety.
